constraint: hexa-only (no host interp rebuild needed; parse-check OK both pre and post)
predecessors: omega-stdlib-3..12 (io / strings / iter / ml / math / dedup / template / process / collection sweeps)

categories_examined: method-side: parse_int, parse_float, to_string, to_int, to_float, to_bool

audit_findings.issue: random_int (L11653) silently returned 0 / distorted range when given non-INT args
audit_findings.rationale: args[0].int_val on a TAG_STR / TAG_VOID returns the raw int_val slot which may be 0 or arena-residual. floor(random()*(hi-lo)) with hi<=lo evaluates to lo silently.

audit_findings.issue: time_elapsed (L11733) accepted any tag via .int_val, producing massive bogus diffs on TAG_STR
audit_findings.rationale: callers passing string timestamps would get timestamp() - <random_int_slot>, which becomes a huge positive number — looks like real elapsed time but is meaningless.
Common pattern format_number(x) without decimals collapses to "0" instead of formatting x with default precision.

audit_findings.issue: Dead duplicate dispatch arms parse_int (L13632) / parse_float (L13638)
audit_findings.rationale: Fast-path L13486/L13493 wins first; the bodies at L13632/L13638 are unreachable (host to_int/to_float vs. .parse_int divergence is irrelevant).
audit_findings.verdict: LEFT IN PLACE — removal would mirror the L13602 'dead duplicates removed' precedent but adds no observable change. Documented for future cleanup pass.

audit_findings.issue: parse_int builtin (L13486) and string method .parse_int (L16139) silently return 0 on garbage strings
audit_findings.verdict: INTENTIONAL contract divergence vs strict to_int (which exec_throws). Callers that want strictness should use to_int; parse_* is by-design lenient. NOT changed.

audit_findings.issue: to_i64 alias (L9960) catches throws and returns 0
audit_findings.verdict: DOCUMENTED in hxa-20260424-006 comment — correct semantics.

audit_findings.issue: sleep_s (L11744) passes raw args[0] to host sleep_s without tag-narrow
audit_findings.verdict: Host primitive accepts INT/FLOAT; no observed crash in field. Left as-is.

audit_findings.issue: rand_2d (L15894) — seed_ref writeback occurs only if args[2].tag == TAG_ARRAY
audit_findings.verdict: Correctly guarded; happy path uses ARRAY-wrapped seed.

audit_findings.issue: randn (L11646) — Box-Muller with safe_u1 floor at 1e-6

fixes_applied.guard: args[0].tag != TAG_INT || args[1].tag != TAG_INT -> val_int(0); hi <= lo -> val_int(lo)
1-arg form now formats with decimals=0 instead of collapsing to literal "0"; decimals tag-checked via if-expr default

fixes_deferred.reason: Dead duplicates — already shadowed by L13486/L13493 fast-path. Removal is cosmetic; defer to next dedup-style cycle (mirroring stdlib-8 pattern).
Host primitive tolerates INT/FLOAT; no observed bug; over-guarding could break callers passing TAG_FLOAT seconds.

validation.parse_check_pre: OK: hexa parse self/hexa_full.hexa parses cleanly
validation.parse_check_post: OK: hexa parse self/hexa_full.hexa parses cleanly (after 3 guard insertions)
validation.host_rebuild: NOT NEEDED (interpretive arms only; no AST/parser change)
validation.regression_risk: LOW — guards only widen reject-input domain to safe defaults. random_int hi<=lo branch now returns lo (previously returned lo+floor(random()*0)=lo too — equivalent). format_number 1-arg path is the only behavioral change (now formats vs returns "0").

anti_spoof_lint_notes: All 3 fixes are guard-additions or default-supplying, not new return paths.
format_number 1-arg behavior change is a CORRECTION (literal "0" was the bug); call sites that relied on the old return are themselves bugs.
next_cycle_proposal: omega-stdlib-14: dead-duplicate sweep (L13632 parse_int, L13638 parse_float, L13644+ to_json, L13650+ from_json) — purely cosmetic but improves dispatch-table comprehensibility.
